mission.title: Install and Configure NATS
mission.description: NATS is a high-performance messaging system designed for cloud-native applications. It is ideal for microservices architectures, IoT, and real-time data streaming. Key trade-offs include simplicity and performance versus advanced messaging features found in other systems.

steps.title: Add the NATS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official NATS Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install NATS easily.

```bash

helm repo add nats https://nats-io.github.io/k8s/helm/charts

helm repo update

```

> This is the official NATS Helm chart repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install NATS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs NATS in the specified namespace using the Helm chart. It sets the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm install nats nats/nats --namespace nats --create-namespace --version 2.11.0

```

> Installs NATS in the 'nats' namespace.

steps.title: Create a NATS configuration
steps.description:
This step creates a custom configuration for NATS to define its behavior and settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: nats-server
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a NATS server with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: nats-server
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify NATS installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the NATS pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> You should see 3 NATS pods in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NATS
uninstall.description:
This command removes the NATS installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall nats --namespace nats

kubectl delete namespace nats

```

> This removes the NATS release.
> This deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade NATS
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations. This step will upgrade NATS to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

helm upgrade nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 2.11.0

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Backup current NATS configuration.
> Upgrades NATS to the specified version.
> Verify that the NATS pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: NATS pods are in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the NATS configuration file.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the NATS pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n nats' and correct the configuration.

troubleshooting.title: NATS service is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The NATS service may not be properly exposed or the network policy is blocking access.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is of type LoadBalancer or NodePort and check network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Unable to connect to NATS from clients
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Client configuration may not match the server settings.

**Fix:**
Verify the client connection settings and ensure they match the NATS server configuration.

troubleshooting.title: NATS server is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to the NATS pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the NATS deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for JetStream improvements and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all NATS pods showing '1/1' and 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
